commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs human PR handling because the external contributor must provide after-fix real Matrix proof and maintainers must decide whether this partial scope should close or only reference the broader Matrix issue.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff touches Matrix routing logic and focused tests only, with no dependency, workflow, secret-handling, or supply-chain surface. (sha=3218cc69d96d67b632c2e7496c6545c9c94a0a50)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Matrix direct-room classification and tests so a bot member event with `is_direct:false` vetoes strict-DM routing, including the inbound `m.direct` branch and direct-room promotion diagnostics.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reads the bot's explicit `is_direct:false` member flag but still returns strict-DM classification, and the `m.direct` branch returns before checking that local veto. I did not run tests because this was a read-only checkout review.
commands.review_followup: Needs human PR handling because the external contributor must provide after-fix real Matrix proof and maintainers must decide whether this partial scope should close or only reference the broader Matrix issue.

target.title: fix(matrix): honor is_direct=false on bot member event as strict-DM veto
target.body:
Fixes #85017.

## After-fix proof — vitest output on the four acceptance-criteria specs

ClawSweeper's stated acceptance-criteria command:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send/targets.test.ts
```

Output on this branch (`3218cc69`), verbose reporter, host: macOS / Node 24.15 / pnpm 11.1.0:

```
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-room.test.ts > inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence > honors an explicit is_direct: false on the bot's own member event as a strict-DM veto
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-room.test.ts > inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence > treats absent is_direct on the bot's member event as no signal (strict by member count)
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-room.test.ts > inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence > does not retry getUserId when callers explicitly pass a missing self user
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-room.test.ts > inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence > resolves selfUserId when callers leave it undefined

 ✓ |extension-matrix| monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > treats m.direct rooms as DMs
 ✓ |extension-matrix| monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > ignores m.direct mapping when local member state explicitly sets is_direct: false
 ✓ |extension-matrix| monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > does not trust stale m.direct classifications for shared rooms
 ✓ |extension-matrix| monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > treats self is_direct false member state as a non-DM signal
 ✓ |extension-matrix| monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > still rejects recent invite candidates when self member state is_direct is false
 ✓ |extension-matrix| monitor/direct.test.ts > createDirectRoomTracker > does not promote recent invite candidates when local vetoes mark the room as non-DM
   (+ 19 other existing tests in this file, all green)

 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-management.test.ts > promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate > does not classify rooms with local is_direct false as direct
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-management.test.ts > promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate > classifies a strict room as direct and repairs m.direct
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-management.test.ts > promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate > returns already-mapped without rewriting account data
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-management.test.ts > promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate > still classifies the room as direct when repair fails
 ✓ |extension-matrix| direct-management.test.ts > inspectMatrixDirectRooms > does not treat discovered rooms with local is_direct false as active DMs
   (+ 10 other tests in this file, all green)

 ✓ |extension-matrix| send/targets.test.ts > resolveMatrixRoomId > uses m.direct when available
 ✓ |extension-matrix| send/targets.test.ts > resolveMatrixRoomId > falls back to joined rooms and persists m.direct
 ✓ |extension-matrix| send/targets.test.ts > resolveMatrixRoomId > prefers joined rooms marked direct in local member state over plain strict rooms
   (+ 8 other tests in this file, all green)

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
   Duration  417ms
```

The four critical behaviors the patch is responsible for:

| Test | Status | Behavior asserted |
|---|---|---|
| `direct-room.test.ts > honors an explicit is_direct: false ... as a strict-DM veto` | ✅ NEW | `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence` returns `strict: false` when bot member event has `is_direct: false` |
| `direct-room.test.ts > treats absent is_direct ... as no signal (strict by member count)` | ✅ NEW | Legacy two-member DM classification preserved when no explicit signal |
| `monitor/direct.test.ts > ignores m.direct mapping when local member state explicitly sets is_direct: false` | ✅ NEW | Inbound m.direct branch now honors the local veto |
| `direct-management.test.ts > does not classify rooms with local is_direct false as direct` | ✅ PRESERVED | Re-ordered checks keep `reason: "local-explicit-false"` matching first; existing regression intact |

## Summary

Three minimal patches across the three call sites that decide DM-vs-room routing, so an explicit `is_direct: false` on the bot's own `m.room.member` event vetoes strict-DM classification consistently:

1. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.ts`** — `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence` folds `memberStateFlag === false` into `strict: false` so callers of `isStrictDirectRoom` (helper-level) immediately see the room as a group.

2. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.ts`** — `isDirectMessage`'s `m.direct`-account-data branch now consults `resolveDirectMemberFlag` and short-circuits to `false` when the local member state is explicit-false. Without this the inbound tracker still returned `true` for `m.direct`-mapped rooms before any local veto could fire — exactly the gap ClawSweeper flagged at `monitor/direct.ts:221`. The legacy "treats m.direct rooms as DMs" behavior is preserved when local state is `true` or absent.

3. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.ts`** — `promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate` re-orders its checks so `memberStateFlag === false → reason: "local-explicit-false"` is matched before the generic `!strict → reason: "not-strict"`. Preserves the existing regression test contract at `direct-management.test.ts:281`.

## Scope NOT covered (deliberately, for a follow-up PR)

ClawSweeper's review noted that the broader fix would also veto strict-DM routing for **named rooms** (`m.room.name` present) and **rooms explicitly listed in `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>]`**, since those are also clear user-intent signals.

Important Synapse behavior verified during reproduction: **`/createRoom is_direct: false` does NOT propagate to member events** — Synapse only adds `is_direct: true` to invites when set true; absent otherwise. Verified against `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/matrix:latest` (Synapse latest) on a live Tailscale-served deployment: created a room with `is_direct: false`, the bot's `m.room.member` event came back with `is_direct: null`, not `false`.

So rooms created via the explicit-false API don't actually carry the signal this PR honors; they need name-based or config-based vetoes to be fully covered. Both of those vetoes touch additional config-plumbing surface and felt out of scope for an issue-author PR that's already changing routing precedence. Maintainers can land these three patches first and follow up.

## Live-environment reproduction (pre-fix)

Verified end-to-end on the deployment that motivated #85017 (Unraid host, `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest`, `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/matrix:latest` AIO Synapse, Tailscale tailnet):

- Created `#parents` via `/createRoom` with `is_direct: false`, named, E2EE on, bot invited. Bot joins → 2 members.
- With `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>] = { requireMention: true, skills: [...] }` set, posted a **plain message (no `@openclaw` mention)**: bot replied. `requireMention` ignored — the bug.
- Added a 3rd user (`@admin`) → 3 members → `isStrictDirectMembership` returns `false` → re-posted plain message: bot ignored. Re-posted with `@openclaw` mention: bot replied in a thread (per channel-level `threadReplies: "always"`).
- Toggled `dm.enabled`, moved per-room config between `rooms` and `groups`, set `requireMention: true` everywhere, etc. — none changed the behavior in 2-member rooms. The 3rd-member workaround is the only thing that makes `requireMention` honored today.

Confirms member-count is the sole gate in the unpatched code. The unit tests above codify the new explicit-`is_direct: false` veto path.

## Related

- #57124 — earlier partial fix for "discovered DM candidates"; this PR layers the same intent onto the three runtime classification paths.
- #85017 — the filed issue with full reproduction.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
